channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Meta’s Q4 profits exceed Wall Street’s expectations
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. posted sharply higher profit and revenue for its fourth quarter on Wednesday, thanks to higher ad revenue on its social media properties, sending its shares up in after-hours trading even as it forecast increasing expenses on its artificial intelligence efforts.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: John Mulaney dunked on Meghan Markle and Prince Harry at star-studded Netflix event
item.description: The comedian poked fun at Meghan and Harry's Netflix track record, shortly after the duchess announced she was delaying the release of her lifestyle show in the wake of the Los Angeles wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Martha Ross, The Mercury News

item.title: Apple's iPhone sales during the holiday season slipped despite a highly anticipated AI rollout
item.description: Apple on Thursday disclosed its iPhone sales dipped slightly during the holiday-season quarter, signaling a sluggish start to the trendsetting company’s effort to catch up to the rest of Big Tech in the race to bring artificial intelligence to the masses.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US cybersecurity agency's future role in elections remains murky under the Trump administration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The nation’s cybersecurity agency has played a critical role in helping states shore up the defenses of their voting systems, but its election mission appears uncertain amid sustained criticism from Republicans and key figures in the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Coming soon
item.description: The Deepseek-R1 model has managed to attract a lot of attention in a short time, especially because it can be used commercially without restrictions.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI Pact: Simplifying EU AI Act compliance for enterprises
item.description: While most provisions of the EU AI Act come into effect at the end of a two-year transition period ending in August 2026, some of them enter force as early as February 2, 2025.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Amazon sues state agency to block release of company records to Bezos-owned Washington Post
item.description: Amazon is suing a Washington state agency to prevent the release of some company materials to The Washington Post, the newspaper which is owned by Amazon founder Jeff Bezos.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Civ 7 requirements for PC, Steam Deck, Linux, and Mac
item.description: Here are the Civ 7 requirements for PC, Mac, and Steam Deck. This includes minimum and recommended PC specs for the game so that you can see whether you can run it.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Larry Magid: Testing DeepSeek, the Chinese generative AI app
item.description: Based on my tests and published reports, DeepSeek not yet as advanced as its American counterparts, but it’s quite good
item.dc:creator: Larry Magid, The Mercury News

item.title: The Nothing Phone 3a series is confirmed for March 4, and a photo and specs rumor gives us an idea of what to expect
item.description: The Nothing Phone 3a is landing on March 4, possibly alongside the 3a Pro, and a hands-on photo gives us a glimpse at one of these devices.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: PSN accounts will now be optional for some PlayStation games on PC, but there will be 'incentives' for users who link them
item.description: Sony has announced it will be dropping the PlayStation Network account requirement for some PC versions of its games.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How to make sure you’ve got the cybersecurity people you need
item.description: The government's personnel shop has devised a five-part strategy for planning, hiring and retaining cybersecurity people. But not everyone follows the strategy. The post How to make sure you’ve got the cybersecurity people you need first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Most in new poll disapprove of Musk's role in Trump administration
item.description: Most registered voters disapprove of tech billionaire Elon Musk’s role in the Trump administration, according to a poll released Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Did DeepSeek copy ChatGPT to make new AI chatbot? Trump adviser thinks so
item.description: Did the upstart Chinese tech company DeepSeek copy ChatGPT to make the artificial intelligence technology that shook Wall Street this week? That's what ChatGPT maker OpenAI is suggesting, along with U.S. President Donald Trump's top AI adviser.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: DeepSeek marks 'turning point' for global AI race: Former Google CEO
item.description: Former Google CEO Eric Schmidt said Tuesday that the rise of DeepSeek, the Chinese startup behind a new artificial intelligence (AI) model taking the internet by storm, marks a “turning point” for the global AI race. In a Washington Post op-ed with MakerMaker.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Don’t be so quick to click that Google Calendar invite. It could be a hacker’s trap
item.description: A phishing campaign uses Google Calendar to schedule fake meeting invitations that appear legitimate, redirecting targets to phishing sites.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The AI bust is here
item.description: Hands up! Who remembers the dot.com bust? I do. It was March 10, 2000. The NASDAQ Composite index peaked at 5,048.62 points. That doesn’t sound like much today, but it was up 800% from 1995. Then people started to get nervous about the true value of the dot.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 3 exceptional text expansion tools for Windows
item.description: Do you find yourself typing the same things over and over? If the answer is yes — and if you type enough text during the workweek, it almost certainly will be — I’d highly recommend you install a text expansion application.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 11 advanced Android split-screen tricks
item.description: It’s fascinating to see how few people actively use some of Android’s most useful features. I had such a revelation around the platform’s long-standing split-screen option this month.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: DashCon, the infamous celebration of Tumblr fandom, is coming back. This time, organizers say it will be different
item.description: Anyone well-versed in internet lore will be familiar with the image of a slightly deflated child’s ball pit on a hotel convention room floor. DashCon (originally called Tumblr-Con, although not officially affiliated with Tumblr) was a fan convention held in Schaumburg, Illinois, back in 2014.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: New Vatican document offers AI guidelines from warfare to health care
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — A Vatican document released Tuesday offers wide-ranging ethical guidelines for the application of artificial intelligence in sectors from warfare to health care, with an underlying call that the burgeoning technology must be used as a tool to complement, and not replace, human in
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: EY’s own experience with AI provides important lessons
item.description: Anja Allen, a principal at EY, said the internal rollout of Microsoft Copilot offered insights into what goes into successful AI implementations. The post EY’s own experience with AI provides important lessons first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Federal employee lawsuit alleges security, privacy risks in new OPM communications system
item.description: The plaintiffs claim OPM violated the E-Government Act by not releasing details of how the email system will manage federal employees’ personal information. The post Federal employee lawsuit alleges security, privacy risks in new OPM communications system first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Drew Friedman, Federal News Network

item.title: IBM targets mainframe customers with prebuilt AI training modules
item.description: IBM is rolling out a portfolio of enterprise data sets aimed at jumpstarting AI development for its Z and LinuxONE mainframe customers.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: DeepSeek's new AI chatbot and ChatGPT answer sensitive questions about China differently
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Chinese tech startup DeepSeek ’s new artificial intelligence chatbot has sparked discussions about the competition between China and the U.S. in AI development, with many users flocking to test the rival of OpenAI's ChatGPT. DeepSeek’s AI assistant was the No.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Upstart Chinese AI company DeepSeek's founder started out as a low-key hedge fund entrepreneur
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The 40-year-old founder of China’s DeepSeek, an AI startup that has startled markets with its capacity to compete with industry leaders like OpenAI, kept a low profile as he built up a hedge fund and then refined its quantitative models to branch into artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: DeepSeek triggers shock waves for AI giants, but the disruption won’t last
item.description: Chinese start-up DeepSeek rocked the tech industry Monday after the company’s new generative AI (genAI) bot hit Apple’s App Store and Google Play Store and downloads almost immediately exceeded those of OpenAI’s ChatGPT.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: A distributed state of mind: Event-driven multi-agent systems
item.description: While large language models are useful for chatbots, Q&A systems, translation, and other language tasks, their real power emerges when they can act on insights, automating a broader range of problems. In other words, we unlock their greatest potential when we tap their reasoning capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The crisis of AI’s hidden costs
item.description: The AI gold rush is creating an unexpected problem: massive waste in resource provisioning. Cloud cost overruns inevitably lead to a lack of true ROI for these systems. Many enterprises spend $2 to get $1 of benefits. That dog won’t hunt.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump says Microsoft is one of the companies eyeing TikTok
item.description: President Donald Trump said Monday evening that Microsoft is among the U.S. companies looking to take control of TikTok to help the popular app avert an effective ban that could kick-in in April.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cell service getting worse? One study suggests that it is
item.description: The study, from J.D. Power, showed an increase in reported wireless network problems between the first and the second half of 2024.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, NewsNation

item.title: 11 cutting-edge programming languages to learn now
item.description: A clean slate. A fresh start. The new broom sweeps clean. The folk sayings are in agreement. Sure, your favorite old programming language is comfortable and easy to use. You know the APIs and libraries backward and forward. But you also know all the glitches, bugs, and failure modes that go with it.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How to pick the right SAST tool
item.description: Like pretty much any security tool, picking a SAST (static application security testing) tool isn’t a matter of “What’s the best?” but rather “What’s the best for me?” Whether you already have a tool that needs replacing or you’re starting from greenfield, it’s helpful to understand the market so yo
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Families sue TikTok in France over teen suicides they say are linked to harmful content
item.description: CASSIS, France (AP) — In the moment when her world shattered three years ago, Stephanie Mistre found her 15-year-old daughter, Marie, lifeless in the bedroom where she died by suicide.
item.dc:creator: TOM NOUVIAN, Associated Press, NewsNation
